title: AMI TS3005b LCD remote-control design discussion
expected_context: The team continues the remote-control product design discussion.
The discussion focuses on button behavior, target market, LCD/touchscreen use, cost, power supply, and basic remote-control functions.
The audio is degraded, so wording may be imperfect, but the main design decisions and concerns should still be captured.
expected_decisions: Include a dedicated control for switching between one-digit and two-digit channel entry.
Target the new product primarily at customers younger than forty while not intentionally excluding older customers.
Use an LCD or touchscreen screen as the current design direction.
Keep rubber or physical buttons as a backup plan if the LCD screen is too expensive.
Put teletext and less important settings in separate LCD menus or submenus.
Use automatic standby or shutdown behavior after a short idle period rather than requiring users to manually turn the remote on first.
expected_requirements: The main remote-control functions should include channel selection, volume control, and power.
The remote should include a mute function.
Teletext should still be available for users who want it.
Less important functions such as screen settings, audio settings, and channel settings should be available but not primary.
The LCD or touchscreen should support adjustable on-screen buttons.
The design should consider the 12.50 production-cost constraint.
The design should address power supply and battery usage.
The design should include a low-battery indicator on the screen.
expected_design_ideas: An LCD or touchscreen that displays only the needed buttons.
A minimal screen with core controls such as channel and volume.
A separate LCD menu for teletext.
Submenus for screen, audio, and channel settings.
Physical or rubber buttons as a fallback option.
Rechargeable or refillable power similar to a mouse-style charging approach.
Automatic standby after roughly two or three minutes of inactivity.
A low-battery indicator shown on the screen.

expected_actions.action: Post or share LCD cost information in the project mail folder if cost information is received.

expected_risks: LCD or touchscreen screens may be too expensive for the low-cost production target.
An LCD-only design may not appeal to older users.
LCD screens may drain batteries quickly.
Requiring users to turn the remote on before use could hurt usability.
Different TV models may handle two-digit channel entry differently.
expected_open_questions: How much the LCD or touchscreen screen will cost.
Whether the LCD screen can fit within the 12.50 production-cost constraint.
Whether older users will accept an LCD-heavy remote.
Which controls should remain physical versus placed in LCD menus.
How the final power, charging, standby, and low-battery behavior should work.
resolved_questions: The team agrees to proceed with LCD for now.
The team agrees that buttons should remain the backup plan if LCD is too expensive.
The team aligns that the new target market is younger than forty.
The team agrees the one-digit/two-digit channel-entry control should be included.
acceptable_behavior: Summarize the LCD/touchscreen decision as current direction, not as final production approval.
Capture the backup button plan if LCD is too expensive.
Capture battery, standby, and low-battery concerns.
Capture the Marketing Expert cost-sharing follow-up without inventing a deadline.
Mention degraded audio only as a quality attribute, not as meeting content.
must_not_invent: A final decision to remove all physical buttons
A final decision to target only young users
Facts from outside the first 25-minute clip
